```{r monthly-mode, results='hide', fig.show='hide'}
    Mode   = md,
    Month  = all_months,
    median = apply(M, 2, median),
    lo95   = apply(M, 2, quantile, probs = 0.025),
    hi95   = apply(M, 2, quantile, probs = 0.975)
  )
}) |>
  mutate(Mode  = factor(Mode, levels = modes_lvl),
         Month = factor(Month, levels = all_months),
         across(c(median, lo95, hi95), round))

print(monthly_bymode, n = nrow(monthly_bymode))
write.csv(monthly_bymode, file.path(output_dir, "monthly_estimates_by_mode.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

mode_cols <- c("Shore" = "#2c7fb8", "Private Boat" = "#d95f0e", "Commercial/Charter" = "#31a354")

# (1) Grouped (dodged) bars, one colour per mode, with 95% interval error bars.
p_bymode_dodge <- monthly_bymode |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Month, y = median, fill = Mode)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.75, alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lo95, ymax = hi95),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = mode_cols) +
  labs(title = "Monthly Dungeness Catch by Crabbing Mode",
       subtitle = "Median with 95% interval. Shore: BSS posterior. Private Boat: BSS posterior (all-gear), PE point with design-based effort SE (pot closure). Commercial/Charter: census, catch-per-vessel CV.",
       x = "Month", y = "Dungeness crab caught", fill = "Mode") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), legend.position = "top")
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "plot_monthly_catch_by_mode.png"), p_bymode_dodge, width = 13, height = 6)

# (2) Faceted companion (free y) so shore and commercial panels stay readable
# next to the larger boat panel.
p_bymode_facet <- monthly_bymode |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Month, y = median, fill = Mode)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lo95, ymax = hi95), width = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = mode_cols) +
  facet_wrap(~Mode, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "Monthly Dungeness Catch by Crabbing Mode",
       subtitle = "Median with 95% interval (free y-axis per panel)",
       x = "Month", y = "Dungeness crab caught") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), legend.position = "none")
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "plot_monthly_catch_by_mode_facet.png"), p_bymode_facet, width = 12, height = 9)


# ===========================================================================
```

```{r monthly-mode-show, echo=TRUE, fig.height=7}
for (nm in c('p_bymode_dodge','p_bymode_facet')) if (exists(nm)) print(get(nm))
```

## 9. Catch by crabbing mode

Total season catch attributed to each crabbing mode.

```{r catch-mode, results='hide'}
# 7.9 CATCH BY CRABBING MODE
# ===========================================================================

cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n CATCH BY CRABBING MODE\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")

mode_table <- tibble(
  Mode = c("Shore (Dock/Jetty/Beach)", "Private Boat", "Commercial/Charter", "PORT TOTAL"),
  PE_Catch = c(
    pe_pop_sum("shore", "Dungeness_Kept"),
    pe_pop_sum("private_boat", "Dungeness_Kept"),
    pe_field("comm_charter", "Dungeness_Kept"), NA
  ),
  PE_Effort = c(
    pe_pop_sum("shore", "effort_total"),
    pe_pop_sum("private_boat", "effort_total"),
    pe_field("comm_charter", "effort_total"), NA
  )
)
mode_table$PE_Catch[4] <- sum(mode_table$PE_Catch[1:3], na.rm = TRUE)
mode_table$PE_Effort[4] <- sum(mode_table$PE_Effort[1:3], na.rm = TRUE)
print(mode_table |> mutate(across(where(is.numeric), round)))
write.csv(mode_table, file.path(output_dir, "catch_by_mode.csv"), row.names = FALSE)


# ===========================================================================
```

```{r catch-mode-show, echo=TRUE}
report_table(mode_table, 'Season catch by crabbing mode.')
```

### 9.1 Catch by gear type

In the pooled model, gear-type catch is derived after estimation by allocating each population's total over gear types using the interview gear-catch shares. As of the 2026-07-13 change (item 3 / T2.2) this allocation propagates uncertainty from both sources: the gear shares are drawn from a Dirichlet posterior (`pi ~ Dirichlet(observed gear catch + 0.5)`) and multiplied by the population total's BSS posterior draws, so each gear carries a 95% credible interval rather than a point. It is still an allocation of the pooled total, not an independently modeled per-gear CPUE process (that is the gear-resolved model's role), but its uncertainty is now honest.

```{r catch-gear, results='hide', fig.show='hide'}
# 7.10 CATCH BY GEAR TYPE (Dirichlet pi_gear + BSS total propagation; item 3)
#
# Pooled model: uses interview data to estimate the proportion of catch
# by primary gear type, then applies to combined catch estimates.
# ===========================================================================

cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n CATCH BY GEAR TYPE (proportional)\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")

gear_catch <- dwg$interview |>
  filter(population %in% c("shore","private_boat"), dungeness_kept > 0) |>
  mutate(
    gear_primary = case_when(
      str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)^pot|, pot|pot,") & !str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)slip ring") ~ "Pot",
      str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)ring net")     ~ "Ring Net",
      str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)trap|star")    ~ "Trap",
      str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)snare")        ~ "Snare",
      TRUE                                      ~ "Other"
    )
  )

gear_props <- gear_catch |>
  group_by(population, gear_primary) |>
  summarise(total_catch = sum(dungeness_kept), n_int = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  group_by(population) |>
  mutate(prop_catch = total_catch / sum(total_catch)) |>
  ungroup()

cat("\n  Observed catch proportions by gear type:\n")
print(gear_props |> select(population, gear_primary, n_int, total_catch, prop_catch) |>
        mutate(prop_catch = round(prop_catch, 3)))

total_shore_catch <- pe_pop_sum("shore", "Dungeness_Kept")
total_boat_catch <- pe_pop_sum("private_boat", "Dungeness_Kept")

# --- item 3 (T2.2 / B5, 2026-07-13): propagate pi_gear + total uncertainty -------------
# The old breakdown multiplied POINT interview gear-proportions by a POINT total (a double
# point estimate with no interval). It now propagates BOTH sources: (a) each population's
# gear shares as pi ~ Dirichlet(observed gear catch + 0.5), the multinomial-Dirichlet
# posterior for catch shares; (b) the population total as its BSS posterior draws (the
# summed C_expected draws of that population's fitted sub-seasons, plus the PE point for
# any PE-fallback sub-season). Per-gear catch = total_draw * pi_draw, summed over the two
# populations and the comm/charter census (all Pot), reported as median + 95% CI. NOTE:
# the gear-resolved model has no true Dirichlet either (its pi_gear scaffolding never
# reaches its Stan), so this R-side propagation is the correct fix for both tracks and
# could be back-ported. tryCatch falls back to the old point allocation if a draw object
# is unavailable, so the section can never break the knit.
gear_levels <- c("Pot", "Ring Net", "Trap", "Snare", "Other")

gear_pop_total_draws <- function(pop, n_target) {
  labs <- paste0(pop, "_", vapply(subseasons, function(z) z$name, ""), "_Dungeness_Kept")
  draws <- NULL; pe_add <- 0
  for (lab in labs) {
    b <- bss_all[[lab]]
    if (!is.null(b) && !isTRUE(b$pe_fallback) && !is.null(b$C_exp_draws)) {
      d <- as.numeric(b$C_exp_draws)
      if (is.null(draws)) draws <- d
      else { k <- min(length(draws), length(d)); draws <- draws[seq_len(k)] + d[seq_len(k)] }
    } else {
      pe_add <- pe_add + (pe_all[[sub("_Dungeness_Kept$", "", lab)]]$Dungeness_Kept %||% 0)
    }
  }
  if (is.null(draws)) return(rep(pe_add, n_target))
  draws <- draws + pe_add
  if (length(draws) >= n_target) draws[seq_len(n_target)] else sample(draws, n_target, replace = TRUE)
}

gear_summary <- tryCatch({
  set.seed(params$bss_seed)
  n_gd <- 4000
  rdir <- function(n, alpha) {  # n x length(alpha) Dirichlet draws via gamma normalization
    g <- matrix(rgamma(n * length(alpha), shape = rep(alpha, each = n), rate = 1), nrow = n)
    g / rowSums(g)
  }
  per_gear <- matrix(0, n_gd, length(gear_levels), dimnames = list(NULL, gear_levels))
  for (pop in c("shore", "private_boat")) {
    gc <- gear_props |> dplyr::filter(population == pop)
    counts <- setNames(rep(0, length(gear_levels)), gear_levels)
    if (nrow(gc) > 0) counts[as.character(gc$gear_primary)] <- gc$total_catch
    per_gear <- per_gear + rdir(n_gd, counts + 0.5) * gear_pop_total_draws(pop, n_gd)
  }
  per_gear[, "Pot"] <- per_gear[, "Pot"] + (pe_all$comm_charter$Dungeness_Kept %||% 0)
  tibble(
    gear_primary = gear_levels,
    PE_Catch = round(apply(per_gear, 2, median)),
    lo95     = round(apply(per_gear, 2, quantile, 0.025)),
    hi95     = round(apply(per_gear, 2, quantile, 0.975))
  ) |>
    dplyr::mutate(Proportion = round(PE_Catch / sum(PE_Catch), 3)) |>
    dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(PE_Catch))
}, error = function(e) {
  cat(sprintf("  Gear Dirichlet propagation failed (%s); using point allocation.\n", conditionMessage(e)))
  ge <- gear_props |>
    mutate(pop_total = dplyr::case_when(population == "shore" ~ total_shore_catch,
                                        population == "private_boat" ~ total_boat_catch),
           est_catch = round(pop_total * prop_catch)) |>
    dplyr::select(gear_primary, est_catch)
  ge <- bind_rows(ge, tibble(gear_primary = "Pot",
                             est_catch = round(pe_all$comm_charter$Dungeness_Kept %||% 0)))
  ge |> group_by(gear_primary) |>
    summarise(PE_Catch = sum(est_catch), .groups = "drop") |>
    mutate(lo95 = NA_real_, hi95 = NA_real_, Proportion = round(PE_Catch / sum(PE_Catch), 3)) |>
    arrange(desc(PE_Catch))
})

cat("\n--- Catch by Gear Type (Dirichlet pi_gear + BSS-total propagation) ---\n")
print(gear_summary)
write.csv(gear_summary, file.path(output_dir, "catch_by_gear_type.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

p_gear <- gear_summary |>
  mutate(gear_primary = fct_reorder(gear_primary, PE_Catch)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = gear_primary, y = PE_Catch, fill = gear_primary)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lo95, ymax = hi95), width = 0.2, na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%s (%.0f%%)", format(PE_Catch, big.mark=","), Proportion*100)),
            hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Estimated Dungeness Catch by Gear Type (Dirichlet pi_gear + BSS total)",
       x = "Gear Type", y = "Estimated Catch (median, 95% CI)") +
  theme_bw() + theme(legend.position = "none") + coord_flip()
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "plot_catch_by_gear_type.png"), p_gear, width = 10, height = 6)


```

```{r catch-gear-show, echo=TRUE, fig.height=6, fig.cap='Catch by gear type.'}
report_table(gear_summary, 'Season catch by gear type: Dirichlet-propagated interview gear-shares times BSS population-total draws (median, 95% CI). An NA interval marks the point-allocation fallback.'); if (exists('p_gear')) print(p_gear)
```

## 10. Convergence report

The per-fit diagnostic table behind the gate decision: R-hat and effective sample size for the summed effort and catch, the divergent fraction, and the pass/fail flag. **Healthy:** `pass_convergence` TRUE with R-hat near 1.00 and effective sample size well above 400 on every fit that had sufficient data.

```{r convergence-report, results='hide'}
# ---- 7.11 CONVERGENCE REPORT ----
# v7.0 (B1.8): the pass/fail decision and every diagnostic column are computed once
# in 7.6b (bss_compute_gate) and stored as bss_all[[label]]$gate_info, so this section
# is now pure assembly. This guarantees the report agrees with the method actually
# used in the totals (7.7/7.8/7.8b consume the same use_bss flag). All gate
# warnings were already emitted in 7.6b at fit time. Columns now include the
# scale-aware impact (impact_C_sd, impact_E_sd; the gating criterion) and retain
# the legacy level-distortion (distortion_C, distortion_E) as reported-only.
cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n CONVERGENCE REPORT\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")

convergence_df <- bind_rows(lapply(names(bss_all), function(label) bss_all[[label]]$gate_info))
print(convergence_df)
write.csv(convergence_df, file.path(output_dir, "convergence_report.csv"), row.names = FALSE)


```

```{r convergence-report-show, echo=TRUE}
# Curated, HTML-friendly view of the convergence report: short fit labels and the key
# gate columns only. The full ~20-column table (treedepth, level-distortion, and the
# per-criterion pass flags) is written to convergence_report.csv.
conv_disp <- tryCatch(
  dplyr::transmute(convergence_df,
    Fit             = short_fit(fit),
    AR              = ar_resolution,
    Div             = divergences,
    `Div frac`      = round(divergence_fraction, 3),
    `R-hat C`       = round(C_sum_rhat, 3),
    `R-hat E`       = round(E_sum_rhat, 3),
    `min n_eff`     = pmin(C_sum_neff, E_sum_neff),
    `Impact C (SD)` = round(impact_C_sd, 3),
    `Impact E (SD)` = round(impact_E_sd, 3),
    Pass            = pass_convergence,
    Method          = method_selected),
  error = function(e) convergence_df)
report_table(conv_disp,
  paste("Per-fit convergence summary (key gate columns; full detail in",
        "convergence_report.csv). Div frac is the divergent-transition fraction (backstop",
        "0.05); Impact C/E (SD) is the scale-aware gating criterion (threshold 0.10);",
        "Method is what the totals actually use. Healthy: R-hat near 1.00, min n_eff",
        "above 400, Pass = TRUE."),
  digits = 3)
```

### 10.1 Per-fit BSS plots

Per-component season-total posterior densities (C_sum, E_sum), the BSS daily effort ribbon, and the BSS daily expected-catch ribbon, one set per fitted component. Each is also saved as a PNG in the run folder.

```{r bss-fit-plots-show, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Per-fit BSS effort, catch, and posterior-density plots (one set per fitted component)."}
# Embed the per-fit BSS plots the fit loop ggsave'd. The fit loop runs fig.show='hide'
# and overwrites each plot object every iteration, so the objects do not survive the loop
# for on-page printing; the saved PNGs do. This surfaces them (posteriors, effort, catch)
# in the HTML, matching the gear-resolved report.
bss_fit_pngs <- list.files(output_dir,
  pattern = "^plot_bss_(effort|catch|posteriors)_.*\\.png$", full.names = TRUE)
if (length(bss_fit_pngs)) knitr::include_graphics(sort(bss_fit_pngs)) else
  cat("_No per-fit BSS plots found (no fitted components this run)._")
```

## 11. Model diagnostics

Per-fit diagnostics are written to the run folder: structural-parameter summaries, divergence localization, and posterior-predictive calibration (PPC/PIT). **Healthy:** PPC coverage near nominal (about 0.95 at the 95 percent level) and PIT means near 0.5. These files are the deeper check behind the gate.

```{r diag-perfit, results='hide'}
# ---- 7.12 PER-FIT MODEL DIAGNOSTICS (B1.6) ----
# Additive per-fit diagnostics: structural parameters, divergence localization,
# and posterior predictive calibration. write_bss_diagnostics() is sourced from
# 03_R_functions/model_diagnostics.R and is fully tryCatch-wrapped, so a diagnostic
# failure prints a note and never interrupts the run. Storing bss_data with each
# fit (Section 7) is what enables the PPC; without it the PPC is skipped cleanly.
cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n MODEL DIAGNOSTICS\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
for(label in names(bss_all)) {
  b <- bss_all[[label]]
  if(is.null(b$fit)) next                  # PE-only entry: nothing to diagnose
  cat(sprintf("  %s:\n", label))
  write_bss_diagnostics(b$fit,
                        if(!is.null(b$bss_data)) b$bss_data else NULL,
                        label, output_dir)
}


```

```{r diag-perfit-show, echo=FALSE}
# Surface the posterior-predictive calibration written above so the health check is on
# the page, not only in ppc_calibration_<fit>.csv. Healthy: 95% coverage near 0.95 and
# PIT mean near 0.50; a coverage far from nominal or a PIT far from 0.50 flags a
# mis-calibrated (usually over-dispersed) predictive for that data stream.
ppc <- read_fit_outputs("ppc_calibration")
ppc_disp <- if (!is.null(ppc)) tryCatch(
  dplyr::transmute(ppc,
    Fit         = fit_lbl,
    Stream      = data_type,
    `Cover 50%` = round(coverage_50, 2),
    `Cover 95%` = round(coverage_95, 2),
    `PIT mean`  = round(pit_mean, 2),
    `PIT sd`    = round(pit_sd, 2),
    n           = n_obs),
  error = function(e) NULL) else NULL
report_table(ppc_disp,
  paste("Posterior-predictive calibration by fit and data stream. Healthy: Cover 95%",
        "near 0.95 and PIT mean near 0.50; coverage well above nominal means the",
        "predictive is too wide (over-dispersed), the effort streams are the known case."))
```

### 11.1 Effort over-dispersion decomposition

Splits each effort-count predictive variance into its Poisson floor, negative-binomial observation over-dispersion, and latent-process parts, written per fit. It identifies which lever drives any excess effort variance before a prior or model change is considered.

```{r diag-overdisp, results='hide'}
# ---- 7.13 EFFORT OVER-DISPERSION DECOMPOSITION (T1.5) ----
# Additive per-fit diagnostic. The v7.0 PPC showed the negative-binomial
# effort model is over-dispersed (gear/trailer PIT central humps, coverage_50
# ~ 0.63-0.75 vs the nominal 0.50); that says the effort predictive is too
# wide but not which part of the model to tune. This decomposes each effort-
# count predictive variance, by the law of total variance, into its Poisson
# floor E[mu], the NB observation over-dispersion E[mu^2/r_E] (the r_E /
# sigma_r_E lever), and the latent process variance Var(mu) (the sigma_eps_E /
# single-cell level lever), and reports their shares so the lever is identified
# before any prior or model change. write_effort_overdispersion_diag() is
# sourced from 03_R_functions/diagnose_effort_overdispersion.R and is fully
# tryCatch-wrapped; it writes only CSVs (effort_overdispersion_decomp_<label>
# and ..._byobs_<label>) and changes nothing in the model. It needs the in-
# memory fit and its bss_data (stored with each fit in Section 7), which is why
# it runs here in the fit session rather than from the saved summary CSVs.
cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n EFFORT OVER-DISPERSION DECOMPOSITION (T1.5)\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
for(label in names(bss_all)) {
  b <- bss_all[[label]]
  if(is.null(b$fit)) next                  # PE-only entry: nothing to decompose
  write_effort_overdispersion_diag(b$fit,
                                   if(!is.null(b$bss_data)) b$bss_data else NULL,
                                   label, output_dir)
}


```

```{r diag-overdisp-show, echo=FALSE}
# Surface the effort over-dispersion decomposition written above. Shares sum to 1 and
# name the lever for any excess effort variance: the Poisson floor, NB observation
# over-dispersion (r_E / sigma_r_E), or latent AR-process variance (sigma_eps_E).
od <- read_fit_outputs("effort_overdispersion_decomp")
od_disp <- if (!is.null(od)) tryCatch(
  dplyr::transmute(od,
    Fit           = fit_lbl,
    Stream        = data_type,
    Poisson       = round(share_poisson, 2),
    `NB overdisp` = round(share_nb_overdisp, 2),
    `Latent AR`   = round(share_latent, 2),
    `Cover 50%`   = round(ppc_coverage_50, 2)),
  error = function(e) NULL) else NULL
report_table(od_disp,
  paste("Effort-count predictive variance decomposition (shares sum to 1): Poisson floor,",
        "negative-binomial observation over-dispersion, and latent AR-process variance.",
        "The dominant share is the lever to tune if the effort predictive is too wide."))
```

### 11.2 Extended per-fit outputs and PSIS-LOO

The extended output series (parameter posteriors, AR paths, coverage maps, PPC residuals, sampler diagnostics, prior-vs-posterior tables) and PSIS-LOO cross-validation are written to the run folder for later analysis; nothing here changes an estimate.

```{r diag-extended, results='hide'}
# ---- 7.13b EXTENDED RUN OUTPUTS (O1-O11) ----
# Additive persistence of in-memory quantities not previously saved, so the R
# workspace is rarely needed for later diagnosis. Per-fit (O1-O6, O8, O9): full
# parameter posterior summary, AR latent path, AR-period data coverage, modeled
# daily CPUE, per-observation PPC residuals (exact randomized PIT), HMC sampler
# diagnostics (E-BFMI), summed-quantity draws, prior-vs-posterior. Run-level (O7,
# O10, O11): monthly PE-vs-BSS by mode, gear proportions, per-fit data summary.
# Sourced from 03_R_functions/save_run_diagnostics.R; every writer is tryCatch-
# wrapped and writes only CSVs, so this changes no estimate. The fit objects
# (O12) are deliberately not saved, to avoid output/ bloat.
cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n EXTENDED RUN OUTPUTS (O1-O11)\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
for(label in names(bss_all)) {
  b <- bss_all[[label]]
  if(is.null(b$fit)) next                  # PE-only entry: nothing to extract
  write_fit_extended_diagnostics(b$fit, b$bss_data, b$days_ss, label, output_dir)
  write_loo_diagnostics(b$fit, b$bss_data, b$days_ss, label, output_dir)
  # POOL-5: CPUE estimator triad, saturation exponent, effort-linearity, and the
  # E/h unit assertion (03_R_functions/bss_cpue_diagnostics.R). Diagnostic-only:
  # writes cpue_estimators_/cpue_saturation_/cpue_linearity_<label>.csv and changes
  # no estimate. This is the check that surfaces a boat/shore effort-unit defect
  # (e.g. catch flat in soak time) the same way it did for gear-resolved. Wrapped
  # so a diagnostic error (or the unit assertion) can never abort the run.
  tryCatch(write_cpue_diagnostics(b, label, output_dir),
           error = function(e)
             cat(sprintf("  CPUE diagnostics skipped for %s: %s\n", label, conditionMessage(e))))
}
write_run_level_diagnostics(bss_all, pe_all,
                            if(exists("gear_props")) gear_props else NULL,
                            params, output_dir)


```

```{r diag-extended-show, echo=FALSE}
# CPUE effort-unit validity, per-fit data coverage, and PSIS-LOO, read back from the
# CSVs written above so the key diagnostics render on the page instead of only on disk.

# (a) CPUE estimator triad + linearity + saturation: is the effort unit harvest-valid?
.est <- read_fit_outputs("cpue_estimators")
.lin <- read_fit_outputs("cpue_linearity")
.sat <- read_fit_outputs("cpue_saturation")
cpue_disp <- if (!is.null(.est)) tryCatch({
  d <- dplyr::select(.est, fit_lbl, effort_unit, n_obs, cpue_ratio_of_sums, cpue_model_implied)
  if (!is.null(.lin)) d <- dplyr::left_join(d, dplyr::select(.lin, fit_lbl, beta_h, linearity_flag), by = "fit_lbl")
  if (!is.null(.sat)) d <- dplyr::left_join(d, dplyr::select(.sat, fit_lbl, sat_beta = beta, saturation_flag), by = "fit_lbl")
  dplyr::transmute(d,
    Fit          = fit_lbl,
    Unit         = effort_unit,
    n            = n_obs,
    `CPUE RoS`   = round(cpue_ratio_of_sums, 2),
    `CPUE model` = round(cpue_model_implied, 2),
    beta_h       = round(beta_h, 2),
    `linear?`    = dplyr::if_else(linearity_flag, "FLAG", "ok", missing = "-"),
    `sat beta`   = round(sat_beta, 2),
    `sat?`       = dplyr::if_else(saturation_flag, "FLAG", "ok", missing = "-"))
}, error = function(e) NULL) else NULL
report_table(cpue_disp,
  paste("CPUE effort-unit validity. beta_h is the catch-vs-effort elasticity (harvest-",
        "unbiased when it covers 1, shown as 'linear? = ok'); the model CPUE should track",
        "the design-based ratio-of-sums (RoS). 'sat? = FLAG' (catch sub-linear in soak",
        "time) is expected for pots and is the reason the unit is deployments, not hours."))

# (b) Per-fit data coverage: how much the BSS must lean on the process model.
.fds <- tryCatch(utils::read.csv(file.path(output_dir, "fit_data_summary.csv"),
                                 stringsAsFactors = FALSE), error = function(e) NULL)
fds_disp <- if (!is.null(.fds)) tryCatch(
  dplyr::transmute(.fds,
    Fit          = short_fit(fit),
    Method       = method,
    AR           = ar_resolution,
    Days         = D,
    `Eff obs`    = n_effort_obs,
    Interviews   = n_interviews,
    `I/E obs`    = n_ie_obs,
    `% days eff` = round(pct_days_with_effort, 0),
    `% days int` = round(pct_days_with_interview, 0)),
  error = function(e) NULL) else NULL
report_table(fds_disp,
  paste("Per-fit data coverage: latent days, effort observations, interviews, and I/E",
        "observations, with the percent of days carrying any effort/interview data. Thin",
        "coverage is where the BSS leans hardest on the AR process and priors."))

# (c) PSIS-LOO by stream: predictive fit and reliability (Pareto k).
.loo <- read_fit_outputs("loo_summary")
loo_disp <- if (!is.null(.loo)) tryCatch(
  dplyr::transmute(.loo,
    Fit             = fit_lbl,
    Stream          = stream,
    n               = n_obs,
    elpd_loo        = round(elpd_loo, 1),
    p_loo           = round(p_loo, 1),
    `Pareto k>0.7`  = `n_pareto_k_gt_0.7`),
  error = function(e) NULL) else NULL
report_table(loo_disp,
  paste("PSIS-LOO by data stream. p_loo is the effective number of parameters; a large",
        "'Pareto k>0.7' count means LOO is unreliable for that stream (the model is near-",
        "saturated there), so read its elpd_loo with caution."))
```

### 11.4 Summer-extrapolation transparency (T1.1)

How much of each fitted component's BSS catch falls on days with **no interview**, where the CPUE is imputed by the AR process and priors rather than observed. A high extrapolated share means that component's harvest rests on the model filling unsampled time; it is the transparency counterpart to the summer-dominance caveat, and the place to be most cautious about the headline number. The share is computed from summed daily medians, so read it as a coverage diagnostic, not a re-estimate of the total.

```{r extrapolation-transparency, echo=FALSE}
# T1.1: decompose each fit's BSS catch into interview-days vs extrapolated (no
# interview) days, from the persisted daily catch (bss_daily_catch_*) and daily CPUE
# (bss_daily_cpue_*, which carries n_interviews) outputs. A day is "extrapolated" when
# it has zero interviews, so its CPUE comes entirely from the AR process and priors.
# Diagnostic only; writes extrapolation_transparency.csv.
.dc <- read_fit_outputs("bss_daily_catch")
.dq <- read_fit_outputs("bss_daily_cpue")
extrap_tbl <- NULL
if (!is.null(.dc) && !is.null(.dq) && "n_interviews" %in% names(.dq)) {
  extrap_tbl <- tryCatch({
    dplyr::left_join(
      dplyr::transmute(.dc, fit_lbl, event_date = as.character(event_date), catch = .data[["median"]]),
      dplyr::transmute(.dq, fit_lbl, event_date = as.character(event_date), n_interviews),
      by = c("fit_lbl", "event_date")) |>
      dplyr::mutate(n_interviews = dplyr::coalesce(n_interviews, 0)) |>
      dplyr::group_by(Fit = fit_lbl) |>
      dplyr::summarise(
        `days total`         = dplyr::n(),
        `days sampled`       = sum(n_interviews > 0),
        `catch total`        = round(sum(catch, na.rm = TRUE)),
        `catch extrapolated` = round(sum(catch[n_interviews == 0], na.rm = TRUE)),
        .groups = "drop") |>
      dplyr::mutate(
        `% days sampled`       = round(100 * `days sampled` / `days total`, 0),
        `% catch extrapolated` = round(100 * `catch extrapolated` / `catch total`, 1))
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
}
if (!is.null(extrap_tbl) && nrow(extrap_tbl) > 0) {
  port_extrap_pct <- round(100 * sum(extrap_tbl$`catch extrapolated`) / sum(extrap_tbl$`catch total`), 1)
  utils::write.csv(extrap_tbl, file.path(output_dir, "extrapolation_transparency.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  report_table(extrap_tbl,
    paste0("Share of each fitted component's BSS catch on days with no interview (CPUE ",
           "imputed by the AR process). Across the fitted components, ", port_extrap_pct,
           "% of the summed BSS catch falls on extrapolated days. A high '% catch ",
           "extrapolated' flags a component whose harvest rests on the model filling ",
           "unsampled time; shares use summed daily medians, so read as a coverage ",
           "diagnostic, not a re-estimate of the total."))
} else {
  cat("_Extrapolation transparency unavailable (daily catch / CPUE outputs not found)._")
}
```

## 12. Effort and CPUE multipliers

The fitted effort and CPUE structural quantities (day-type effects, the CPUE weekend effect B1_C, and the effort multipliers) that shape the daily series.

```{r multipliers, results='hide'}
# ---- 7.14 EFFORT & CPUE MULTIPLIERS ----
cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n EFFORT & CPUE MULTIPLIERS\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")

multiplier_results <- list()
for(label in names(bss_all)) {
  b <- bss_all[[label]]
  if(is.null(b$pe_fallback) || !b$pe_fallback) {
    B1_draws <- rstan::extract(b$fit, "B1")$B1
    B2_draws <- rstan::extract(b$fit, "B2")$B2
    B1C_draws <- rstan::extract(b$fit, "B1_C_out")$B1_C_out

    weekend_eff_mult <- exp(B1_draws)
    holiday_eff_mult <- exp(B1_draws + B2_draws)
    weekend_cpue_mult <- exp(B1C_draws)

    multiplier_results[[label]] <- tibble(
      fit = label,
      weekend_effort_mult = sprintf("%.2fx (%.2f-%.2f)",
        median(weekend_eff_mult), quantile(weekend_eff_mult, 0.025), quantile(weekend_eff_mult, 0.975)),
      holiday_effort_mult = sprintf("%.2fx (%.2f-%.2f)",
        median(holiday_eff_mult), quantile(holiday_eff_mult, 0.025), quantile(holiday_eff_mult, 0.975)),
      weekend_cpue_mult = sprintf("%.2fx (%.2f-%.2f)",
        median(weekend_cpue_mult), quantile(weekend_cpue_mult, 0.025), quantile(weekend_cpue_mult, 0.975)),
      B1_C_median = round(median(B1C_draws), 3),
      B1_C_lo95 = round(quantile(B1C_draws, 0.025), 3),
      B1_C_hi95 = round(quantile(B1C_draws, 0.975), 3)
    )
  }
}
if(length(multiplier_results) > 0) {
  multiplier_df <- bind_rows(multiplier_results)
  cat("\n  Multipliers relative to weekdays:\n")
  for(i in 1:nrow(multiplier_df)) {
    cat(sprintf("    %s:\n      Weekend effort: %s\n      Holiday effort: %s\n      Weekend CPUE: %s\n",
                multiplier_df$fit[i],
                multiplier_df$weekend_effort_mult[i],
                multiplier_df$holiday_effort_mult[i],
                multiplier_df$weekend_cpue_mult[i]))
  }
  write.csv(multiplier_df, file.path(output_dir, "effort_cpue_multipliers.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
}


```

```{r multipliers-show, echo=TRUE}
if (exists('multiplier_df')) report_table(multiplier_df, 'Fitted effort and CPUE multipliers.')
```

### 12.1 Expansion ratios (R_G and R_G_boat)

The gear-per-crabber ratio (shore) and gear-per-boat-group ratio (boat) that carry effort onto the gear-deployment scale. The R_G posterior below is under the model's data-driven R_G prior by default. For the T1.3 prior-sensitivity analysis the prior is overridable from `run_config.R` (`R_G_prior_mu` / `R_G_prior_sigma`); the console log records whether the prior was data-driven or an override, and this report reflects whichever prior was active for the run. A full sensitivity is a small grid of runs (R_G at 1.0, the empirical value, and 1.5) compared on the port total, not a single knit.

```{r expansion, results='hide'}
# ---- 7.16 R_G AND R_G_boat POSTERIORS ----
cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n EXPANSION RATIOS (R_G, R_G_boat)\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")

expansion_results <- list()
for(label in names(bss_all)) {
  b <- bss_all[[label]]
  if(is.null(b$pe_fallback) || !b$pe_fallback) {
    R_G_draws      <- rstan::extract(b$fit, "R_G")$R_G
    # POOL-1: R_T replaced by R_G_boat (gear per boat group). Both are always-present
    # parameters now; in the population where a ratio is unused it samples its prior.
    R_G_boat_draws <- rstan::extract(b$fit, "R_G_boat")$R_G_boat

    expansion_results[[label]] <- tibble(
      fit = label,
      R_G_interpretation = sprintf("%.2f gear/crabber (%.2f-%.2f)",
        median(R_G_draws), quantile(R_G_draws, 0.025), quantile(R_G_draws, 0.975)),
      R_G_boat_interpretation = sprintf("%.2f gear/group (%.2f-%.2f)",
        median(R_G_boat_draws), quantile(R_G_boat_draws, 0.025), quantile(R_G_boat_draws, 0.975))
    )
  }
}
if(length(expansion_results) > 0) {
  expansion_df <- bind_rows(expansion_results)
  for(i in 1:nrow(expansion_df)) {
    cat(sprintf("  %s: R_G=%s, R_G_boat=%s\n",
                expansion_df$fit[i], expansion_df$R_G_interpretation[i],
                expansion_df$R_G_boat_interpretation[i]))
  }
  write.csv(expansion_df, file.path(output_dir, "expansion_ratios.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
}


```

```{r expansion-show, echo=TRUE}
if (exists('expansion_df')) report_table(expansion_df, 'Expansion-ratio posteriors (R_G, R_G_boat).')
```

## 13. PE vs BSS comparison

Side by side, the design-based PE and the Bayesian BSS for each component. **How to read it:** close agreement is reassuring; a large gap on a component means the two methods disagree there, usually because that component is data-thin, and the BSS interval width tells you how well-identified it is. Since both now run on the same effort unit, a remaining gap is a genuine model disagreement, not a unit artifact.

```{r pe-vs-bss, results='hide'}
# ---- 7.17 PE VS BSS COMPARISON ----
cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n PE VS BSS COMPARISON\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")

comparison <- list()
for(pop in c("shore","private_boat")) {
  for(ss in subseasons) {
    label_cg <- paste0(pop, "_", ss$name, "_Dungeness_Kept")
    pe_label <- paste0(pop, "_", ss$name)
    b <- bss_all[[label_cg]]

    pe_catch <- pe_all[[pe_label]]$Dungeness_Kept %||% 0
    pe_effort <- pe_all[[pe_label]]$effort_total %||% 0

    if(!is.null(b) && (is.null(b$pe_fallback) || !b$pe_fallback)) {
      bss_catch <- b$C_expected_sum["50%"]
      bss_effort <- b$E_sum["50%"]
      catch_diff_pct <- round((bss_catch - pe_catch) / pe_catch * 100, 1)
      effort_diff_pct <- round((bss_effort - pe_effort) / pe_effort * 100, 1)
      conv <- convergence_df |> filter(fit == label_cg)
      method <- if(nrow(conv) > 0 && conv$pass_convergence[1]) "BSS" else "PE"
    } else {
      bss_catch <- NA; bss_effort <- NA
      catch_diff_pct <- NA; effort_diff_pct <- NA
      method <- "PE"
    }

    comparison[[length(comparison)+1]] <- tibble(
      component = paste0(pop, " (", ss$display_name, ")"),
      PE_effort = round(pe_effort), BSS_effort = round(bss_effort),
      effort_diff_pct = effort_diff_pct,
      PE_catch = round(pe_catch), BSS_catch = round(bss_catch),
      catch_diff_pct = catch_diff_pct,
      method_selected = method
    )
  }
}
comparison[[length(comparison)+1]] <- tibble(
  component = "comm_charter (census)",
  PE_effort = round(pe_all$comm_charter$effort_total), BSS_effort = NA, effort_diff_pct = NA,
  PE_catch = round(pe_all$comm_charter$Dungeness_Kept), BSS_catch = NA, catch_diff_pct = NA,
  method_selected = "Census"
)
comparison_df <- bind_rows(comparison)
print(comparison_df)
write.csv(comparison_df, file.path(output_dir, "pe_vs_bss_comparison.csv"), row.names = FALSE)


```

```{r pe-vs-bss-show, echo=TRUE}
report_table(comparison_df, 'Point Estimator versus Bayesian state-space estimate, by component.')
```

## 14. Season summary

Run-level metadata: the season window, sampling coverage, interview count, how many fits passed convergence, and the model configuration used.

```{r season-summary, results='hide'}
# ---- 7.18 SEASON SUMMARY ----
cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n SEASON SUMMARY\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")

season_days <- as.integer(as.Date(params$est_date_end) - as.Date(params$est_date_start) + 1)
total_effort_days <- n_distinct(c(dwg$shore_effort$event_date, dwg$boat_effort$event_date))
total_interview_n <- nrow(dwg$interview)
n_bss_fits <- sum(!sapply(bss_all, function(b) !is.null(b$pe_fallback) && b$pe_fallback))
n_pass <- sum(convergence_df$pass_convergence, na.rm=TRUE)

cat(sprintf("\n  Season: %s to %s (%d days)\n", params$est_date_start, params$est_date_end, season_days))
cat(sprintf("  Effort days sampled: %d (%d%%)\n", total_effort_days, round(total_effort_days/season_days*100)))
cat(sprintf("  Total interviews: %d\n", total_interview_n))
cat(sprintf("  BSS convergence: %d of %d fits passed\n", n_pass, n_bss_fits))

season_summary <- tibble(
  metric = c("Model configuration", "Season start", "Season end", "Season days",
             "Effort days sampled", "Total interviews", "BSS fits attempted", "BSS fits passed",
             "L_effective method", "AR temporal resolution"),
  value = c("Adaptive AR + L uncertainty", params$est_date_start, params$est_date_end,
            as.character(season_days), as.character(total_effort_days),
            as.character(total_interview_n), as.character(n_bss_fits), as.character(n_pass),
            if(!is.null(L_eff_model)) L_eff_model$method else "civil_twilight",
            paste(unique(na.omit(unlist(ar_resolutions))), collapse = ", "))
)
write.csv(season_summary, file.path(output_dir, "season_summary.csv"), row.names = FALSE)


```

```{r season-summary-show, echo=TRUE}
report_table(season_summary, 'Season summary and run configuration.')
```

## 15. Run parameters and timing

Writes the full run parameters and the timing summary to the run folder for reproducibility.

```{r save-params, results='hide'}
# ---- 7.19 SAVE PARAMETERS ----
timer_stop("6_outputs")
writeLines(capture.output(str(params)), file.path(output_dir, "run_parameters.txt"))

# B1.6: session and version capture for reproducibility. Pins package and Stan
# versions and the seed with each output set, so a result is reproducible from
# the committed artifacts.
writeLines(capture.output(sessionInfo()), file.path(output_dir, "session_info.txt"))
cat(sprintf("\nrstan %s; StanHeaders %s; bss_seed %s\n",
            utils::packageVersion("rstan"),
            utils::packageVersion("StanHeaders"),
            params$bss_seed),
    file = file.path(output_dir, "session_info.txt"), append = TRUE)

cat(sprintf("\nAll outputs saved to: %s\n", output_dir))
cat(paste(" ", list.files(output_dir), collapse="\n"), "\n")

# ---- TIMING SUMMARY ----
run_total <- difftime(Sys.time(), run_start_time, units = "mins")
cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n TIMING SUMMARY\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
timing_df <- tibble(
  Section = names(bss_timer_log()),
  Minutes = sapply(bss_timer_log(), function(x) round(as.numeric(x$elapsed), 1))
)
print(timing_df, n = 20)
cat(sprintf("\n  TOTAL RUNTIME: %.1f minutes (%.1f hours)\n",
            as.numeric(run_total), as.numeric(run_total)/60))

# save.image(file = file.path(output_dir, "BSS-GH-pooled-CPUE-model_workspace.RData"))
```

## Appendix: divergence diagnostics (manual)

These are run by hand after a fit to localize any divergences; they are not part of the automated pipeline.

```{r divergence-diagnostics, results='hide'}

# load('output/20260619/pooled-CPUE/BSS-GH-pooled-CPUE-model_workspace.RData')

source(here::here("03_R_functions/divergence_diagnostic.R"))
# Robust to the sub-season set (build_subseasons): iterate over every fitted BSS
# component and skip PE-fallback components that have no Stan fit, rather than
# hardcoding fit labels (which omitted private_boat_ring_net_only and would miss the
# pre/post all-gear keys of a mid-season closure).
for (nm in names(bss_all)) {
  b <- bss_all[[nm]]
  if (is.null(b) || isTRUE(b$pe_fallback) || is.null(b$fit)) next
  cat(sprintf("\n=== Divergence diagnostics: %s ===\n", nm))
  diagnose_divergences(b$fit)
}
```

### 11.3 Divergence localization

Where the divergent transitions (if any) concentrate. **Healthy:** small `|SMD|` across the board, meaning divergences are diffuse and not biasing any particular parameter or total.

```{r divergence-diagnostics-show, echo=FALSE}
# Surface the top divergence-localization drivers written by the per-fit diagnostics.
# Large |SMD| (standardized median difference between divergent and bulk draws) points
# at the parameter/geometry the divergences concentrate in (e.g. a hierarchical funnel).
dl <- read_fit_outputs("divergence_localization")
dl_disp <- if (!is.null(dl) && "smd" %in% names(dl)) tryCatch(
  dl |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(smd)) |>
    dplyr::group_by(fit_lbl) |>
    dplyr::slice_max(abs(smd), n = 3, with_ties = FALSE) |>
    dplyr::ungroup() |>
    dplyr::transmute(Fit = fit_lbl, Parameter = param, SMD = round(smd, 2),
                     `median (bulk)` = round(median_bulk, 2),
                     `median (div)`  = round(median_div, 2)),
  error = function(e) NULL) else NULL
report_table(dl_disp,
  paste("Top divergence-localization drivers per fit (largest |SMD|). A large |SMD| is",
        "where divergent draws depart most from the bulk; it names the geometry to watch.",
        "Small values across the board mean divergences are diffuse, not biasing a total."))
```
